title: fagelia ascendens
intro: Fagelia ascendens, also known as Indian spurge, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its toxic latex and spurge-like appearance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and insect bites. The health benefits of Fagelia ascendens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and improve overall skin health. Fagelia ascendens can be prepared into herbal remedies such as ointments, salves, and infusions, which can be applied topically to alleviate various skin and wound-related issues.
